etc.purpose: Runtime configuration and dev-tooling assets for OpenMeter — not application source code. Houses seed data pipelines (etc/seed) and local proxy configuration (etc/wiretap) used exclusively for development, testing, and demos.

patterns.name: Environment-variable parameterisation with defaults
patterns.description: All runtime values (tokens, URLs, counts) are injected via ${VAR:default} syntax so configs work out-of-the-box without manual edits.

patterns.name: Benthos/Redpanda Connect pipeline skeleton
patterns.description: Each seed stream follows the identical input.generate + bloblang mapping + output.switch pattern; only the bloblang body differs between streams.
patterns.example: input: { generate: { count: 0, interval: ... } } / pipeline: { processors: [{ bloblang: ... }] } / output: { switch: [...] }

patterns.name: CloudEvents 1.0 envelope
patterns.description: Every generated event must include specversion, id (UUID), type, source, subject, time, and datacontenttype. Dimensional attributes go inside data: {}, not at the root.
patterns.example: { specversion: '1.0', id: uuid_v4(), type: 'api-requests', source: 'seeder', subject: ..., time: now(), data: { tokens: ... } }

patterns.name: Contract binding to generated OpenAPI spec
patterns.description: etc/wiretap points contract at the generated api/openapi.yaml (v1 spec), not at v3. Redirects use host.docker.internal, not localhost.

key_file_guides.role: Benthos pipeline entrypoint: imports all stream configs from etc/seed/streams/ and wires observability.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding new streams requires a corresponding entry here; missing imports silently skip that stream.

key_file_guides.role: Reference implementation of the seed stream skeleton for HTTP API request events.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Copy this skeleton verbatim for new event types; deviating from the input.generate + output.switch shape breaks the pipeline.

key_file_guides.role: Shared Benthos metrics/tracing config included by seed.yaml.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not duplicate in individual stream files.

key_file_guides.role: Wiretap proxy config for Docker-local v1 API contract validation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must target api/openapi.yaml (v1), not api/v3/openapi.yaml; redirectURL must use host.docker.internal.

anti_patterns: Hardcoding OPENMETER_TOKEN or OPENMETER_BASE_URL — always use ${VAR:default}
Setting count > 0 in generate — seed streams must run indefinitely
Putting dimensional attributes outside data: {} — they belong in data, not at CloudEvent root
Pointing wiretap contract at api/v3/openapi.yaml — wiretap here validates v1 traffic only
Using localhost instead of host.docker.internal for redirectURL in the wiretap Docker config

decisions.decision: Seed streams share an identical Benthos skeleton with only the bloblang mapping varying
decisions.rationale: Keeps all streams structurally uniform so operators can read, copy, and modify any stream without learning a new pipeline shape.

decisions.decision: Subject space bounded by SEEDER_SUBJECT_COUNT modulo rather than purely random UUIDs
decisions.rationale: Produces a realistic bounded set of subjects (mimicking real tenants) instead of an ever-growing cardinality that would skew meter aggregations in demos.

test.purpose: Integration and cross-domain test suites that require a real PostgreSQL database. Each child owns one domain or cross-domain scenario; all wire services from raw package constructors (never app/common) to avoid import cycles.

patterns.name: BaseSuite embedding for shared billing infrastructure
patterns.description: Tests that need the full billing stack embed test/billing.BaseSuite (or extend it with SubscriptionMixin). BaseSuite provisions Atlas migrations, a unique namespace, MockStreamingConnector, and pkg/clock control.
patterns.example: type MyTestSuite struct { billingtest.BaseSuite } — then access s.BillingService, s.MockStreamingConnector.

patterns.name: TestEnv interface for service access
patterns.description: test/notification, test/app, and test/customer expose a TestEnv interface rather than service structs directly, keeping the wiring details hidden from test methods.

patterns.name: Unique namespace per test method
patterns.description: Each test method generates a unique namespace (ULID or function-name-based) to prevent FK constraint violations when tests share a database.
patterns.example: ns := ulid.Make().String(); // or setupNamespace(t) returning a unique string

patterns.name: Raw package constructors only — no app/common imports
patterns.description: All test wiring constructs services from their direct constructors (adapter.New, service.New) not from Wire provider sets in app/common to avoid import cycles.

patterns.name: clock.SetTime + defer clock.ResetTime for deterministic time
patterns.description: Tests that depend on time call pkg/clock.SetTime at the start and defer clock.ResetTime() to restore global state for parallel tests.
patterns.example: clock.SetTime(t0); defer clock.ResetTime(); // then call service methods

patterns.name: t.Context() over context.Background()
patterns.description: All new test code uses t.Context() (or s.T().Context()) so context cancellation is tied to the test harness lifecycle.

key_file_guides.role: BaseSuite: provisions Atlas migrations, unique namespace, MockStreamingConnector, billing service stack. Extended by most billing tests.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always use BaseSuite rather than manually provisioning these — duplicating setup leads to subtle test isolation bugs.

key_file_guides.role: SubscriptionMixin: adds subscription+plan wiring on top of BaseSuite for tests that exercise billing+subscription flows.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use SubscriptionMixin when a test needs both billing and subscription services; do not wire subscription separately.

key_file_guides.role: Wires the full notification stack including a real Svix client (requires SVIX_HOST env var).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not create a second TestEnv inside a test method — each starts goroutines and DB connections. Always defer env.Close().

key_file_guides.role: Entry point for credits/charges integration tests; uses chargestestutils.NewServices as the canonical charges stack builder.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use createLedgerBackedCustomer for tests requiring ledger accounts, not BaseSuite.CreateTestCustomer.

key_file_guides.role: Wires all subscription+billing deps via direct constructors and subscriptiontestutils.SetupDBDeps. Shared testDeps struct used by all scenario files.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Hardcoded namespace 'test-namespace'. Do not use a different namespace in this package.

key_file_guides.role: TestEnv for app integration tests; wires billing service via InitBillingService helper without app/common.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always call setupNamespace(t) per test method in AppHandlerTestSuite — never share a namespace across methods.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common or any Wire provider set in test files — always wire from raw package constructors to avoid import cycles
Using context.Background() where t.Context() or s.T().Context() is available
Reusing the same namespace, subject key, or customer key across test functions in the same package
Omitting defer clock.ResetTime() after clock.SetTime() — leaves global clock dirty for subsequent parallel tests
Accessing adapter methods directly when the service covers the same operation — bypasses state machine and validation

decisions.decision: Integration tests live in test/* rather than co-located with production code
decisions.rationale: Cross-domain tests import multiple openmeter/* packages; placing them in a neutral test/* tree prevents import cycles and makes the cross-domain dependency explicit.

decisions.decision: Atlas migrations run by default in BaseSuite (not ent.Schema.Create)
decisions.rationale: Running Atlas migrations in tests validates migration files against the live schema, catching drift before CI. TEST_DISABLE_ATLAS exists as an escape hatch only.

decisions.decision: Scenario-based test files in test/subscription rather than table-driven tests
decisions.rationale: Subscription lifecycle scenarios are complex multi-step flows where numbered step comments provide clearer documentation than a flat table of inputs/outputs.

code_examples.scenario: Extending BaseSuite for a billing+subscription integration test
